items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Winners, losers from NBA playoffs: Cavs get back into series, Thunder push Lakers to the brink
items.blurb: Winners, losers from NBA playoffs: Cavs get back into series, Thunder push Lakers to the brink — The NBA playoffs continued on Saturday with two Game 3s.

items.headline: Lakers HC JJ Redick sends message after Game 3 loss to Thunder
items.blurb: Lakers HC JJ Redick sends message after Game 3 loss to Thunder — LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers fell again to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 3 of the Western Conference semifinals on Saturday with a 131-108 final score, and Lakers head coach JJ Redick isn't happy about it.

items.headline: LeBron James, Lakers will have to make NBA history to win series vs. Thunder
items.blurb: LeBron James, Lakers will have to make NBA history to win series vs. Thunder — After Saturday's loss in Game 3 of the Western Conference semifinals, LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers are now down 3-0 in their series against the Oklahoma City Thunder, and to say it's an uphill battle to climb their way out of this hole is an understatement.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham sends critical message after Pistons' Game 3 loss to Cavaliers
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham sends critical message after Pistons' Game 3 loss to Cavaliers — The Detroit Pistons were hoping to go 3-0 in their series against the Cleveland Cavaliers on Saturday in the Eastern Conference semifinals, but instead, the Cavs gave their hometown crowd a show in a 116-109 victory.

items.blurb: NBA Game 3 Highlights: Thunder 131, Lakers 108 — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 23 points and nine assists, while Ajay Mitchell added 24 points and 10 assists to help the Oklahoma City Thunder beat the Los Angeles Lakers 131-108.

One of the first items on the list is talking to start guard Stephen Curry about a contract extension. Curry has one season left on his deal at $62.6 million for the 2026-27 season. items.headline: Steve Kerr, Warriors make major decision regarding their future
items.blurb: Steve Kerr, Warriors make major decision regarding their future — After weeks of speculation and negotiations, the Golden State Warriors and head coach Steve Kerr have a decision on their future together: Kerr is coming back.

items.headline: AP Source: Steve Kerr agrees to new two-year contract to remain head coach of Warriors
items.blurb: AP Source: Steve Kerr agrees to new two-year contract to remain head coach of Warriors

items.headline: Steve Kerr, Warriors Agree To Two-Year Deal
items.blurb: Steve Kerr, Warriors Agree To Two-Year Deal — Steve Kerr and the Golden State Warriors have agreed to a two-year deal for Kerr to remain the team's head coach. Kerr's contract was set to expire at the end of the league year, but he'll now return for his 13th and 14th seasons leading the Warriors. This new two-year deal will keep Kerr as the NBA's highest-paid head coach. Kerr became Golden State's

items.headline: Cavaliers’ Harden sinks back-to-back clutch buckets to seal Game 3 win
items.blurb: Cavaliers’ Harden sinks back-to-back clutch buckets to seal Game 3 win — Watch as Cleveland Cavaliers’ James Harden turns back the clock, sinking back-to-back contested buckets in the clutch, to ice Game 3 against the Detroit Pistons.

items.headline: NBA Game 3 Highlights: Cavaliers 116, Pistons 109
items.blurb: NBA Game 3 Highlights: Cavaliers 116, Pistons 109 — Donovan Mitchell had 35 points and 10 rebounds, James Harden added 19 points with seven assists, and the Cleveland Cavaliers earned a 116-109 win over the Detroit Pistons in Game 3.

items.headline: James Harden re-emerges with huge fourth quarter to get Cavaliers on the board in East semis
items.blurb: James Harden re-emerges with huge fourth quarter to get Cavaliers on the board in East semis — When the Cleveland Cavaliers needed James Harden the most in Game 3, the divisive star delivered.

items.headline: NBA teams finally know story behind Darryn Peterson's cramping issues
items.blurb: NBA teams finally know story behind Darryn Peterson's cramping issues — Few prospects in recent NBA history could equal Kansas' Darryn Peterson. But constant cramping, injuries, and questions about his love for the game might cost him the No. 1 spot in June's 2026 NBA Draft.

items.headline: Cavaliers’ Mobley gets offensive board, throws down slam over Stewart
items.blurb: Cavaliers’ Mobley gets offensive board, throws down slam over Stewart — Watch as Evan Mobley fights for the offensive rebound after a free throw, then throws down the two-handed slam over Isaiah Stewart.

items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo Believed To Want To Play For Eastern Conference Title Contender — This summer, teams are expected to be lining up their best trade offers for Giannis Antetokounmpo. items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo Will Likely Push For A Trade To A Specific Team — On a recent appearance on FanDuel TV's Run It Back Show, Sam Amick of The Athletic talked about the likelihood Giannis Antetokounmpo gets traded from the Milwaukee Bucks. items.headline: Jalen Brunson enhances Knicks' legacy in Game 3 win over 76ers
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson enhances Knicks' legacy in Game 3 win over 76ers — Further enhancing his legacy, Jalen Brunson enjoyed another memorable playoff performance in a New York Knicks uniform on Friday night.

items.headline: Referee Tony Brothers had to be held back in heated Game 3 confrontation with Chris Finch
items.blurb: Referee Tony Brothers had to be held back in heated Game 3 confrontation with Chris Finch — It’s not every day that an NBA referee is the one who needs restraining during a playoff game.

items.headline: Charles Barkley claps back at Draymond Green after on-air insult
items.blurb: Charles Barkley claps back at Draymond Green after on-air insult — There was some awkwardness on the set of "Inside the NBA" this past week when Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green took an unbelievable shot at the end of Charles Barkley's career.

items.headline: LeBron James: Tallies 19 points in Game 3 defeat
items.blurb: LeBron James: Tallies 19 points in Game 3 defeat — James recorded 19 points (7-19 FG, 2-6 3Pt, 3-4 FT), eight assists, six rebounds and one steal over 37 minutes during Saturday's 131-108 loss to the Thunder in Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: 2026 NBA Draft Lottery Primer: Could Thunder land No. 1 pick?
items.blurb: 2026 NBA Draft Lottery Primer: Could Thunder land No. 1 pick? — After a season filled with tanking, some teams could be rewarded at Sunday’s NBA Draft Lottery. Sportsnet’s Steven Loung explains how it will all go down.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: Near double-double in Game 3 win
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: Near double-double in Game 3 win — Gilgeous-Alexander logged 23 points (7-20 FG, 3-7 3Pt, 6-7 FT), nine assists, four rebounds, two blocks and one steal over 33 minutes during Saturday's 131-108 win over the Lakers in Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Ajay Mitchell: Outstanding in Game 3 win
items.blurb: Ajay Mitchell: Outstanding in Game 3 win — Mitchell logged 24 points (10-17 FG, 2-4 3Pt, 2-3 FT), 10 assists, four rebounds and three steals over 30 minutes during the Thunder's 131-108 win over the Lakers in Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals on Saturday. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: NBA Playoff Odds: Knicks, Thunder At 99 Percent To Advance; Spurs Jump To 92 After Game 3 Win — With the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks each winning Game 3 on Friday to push their series leads to 2-1 and 3-0 respectively, the probable Conference Finals teams continues to take shape. items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama Joins Hakeem, Shaq, Kareem With Historic Game 3 In Win At Wolves — Victor Wembanyama delivered one of the greatest individual playoff performances in NBA big man history Friday, scoring 39 points with 15 rebounds and five blocks to lead the San Antonio Spurs past the Minnesota Timberwolves 115-108, giving San Antonio a 2-1 series lead in the Western Conference semifinals. items.headline: Jarred Vanderbilt: Available to play
items.blurb: Jarred Vanderbilt: Available to play — Vanderbilt (finger) is available for Saturday's Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals, Mike Trudell of Spectrum SportsNet reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jalen Williams: Remaining out for Game 3
items.blurb: Jalen Williams: Remaining out for Game 3 — The Thunder list Williams (hamstring) out Saturday against the Lakers in Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals series. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Winners, losers from NBA playoffs: Villanova alums take down Philly
items.blurb: Winners, losers from NBA playoffs: Villanova alums take down Philly — When the second-round series between the Philadelphia 76ers and New York Knicks moved to the City of Brotherly Love, three Knicks who spent their college years in Philly stepped up.

items.headline: Ben McCollum Signs New Six-Year Deal At Iowa
items.blurb: Ben McCollum Signs New Six-Year Deal At Iowa — Iowa head coach Ben McCollum signed a new six-year deal with the school on Friday, sources told ESPN. In his first season with the program, McCollum lead Iowa to their first Elite Eight appearance since 1987. UNC pursued McCollum to fill their head coach vacancy before appointing Michael Malone.

items.headline: Mike Brown Credits Mikal Bridges' Defense With Knicks' Game 3 Win At 76ers
items.blurb: Mike Brown Credits Mikal Bridges' Defense With Knicks' Game 3 Win At 76ers — The New York Knicks beat the Philadelphia 76ers 108-94 to win Game 3 and take a 3-0 series lead. With the Knicks holding the Sixers to just 18 points in the fourth quarter, coach Mike Brown credited Mikal Bridge's defense on Tyrese Maxey for the win. items.headline: Nigeria Hire David Fizdale As Head Coach
items.blurb: Nigeria Hire David Fizdale As Head Coach — David Fizdale was hired as the head coach of Nigeria's men's basketball team on Friday. "It will be fun and exciting calling timeouts and drawing plays up again as a head coach," Fizdale told ESPN Andscape in a phone interview. items.headline: Blazers Interview Assistants Ben Sullivan, Jared Dudley
items.blurb: Blazers Interview Assistants Ben Sullivan, Jared Dudley — The Portland Trail Blazers interviewed Houston Rockets assistant coach Ben Sullivan and Denver Nuggets assistant coach Jared Dudley , sources told Michael Scotto of HoopsHype. items.headline: Wembanyama’s 39 points lead Spurs past Timberwolves in Game 3
items.blurb: Wembanyama’s 39 points lead Spurs past Timberwolves in Game 3 — Victor Wembanyama delivered another masterpiece with 39 points, 15 rebounds and more game-wrecking defence for the San Antonio Spurs, who took a 2-1 lead in the second-round NBA playoff series with a 115-108 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves on Friday night.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama reaches new heights in Game 3 win
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama reaches new heights in Game 3 win — If the Spurs get this Wembanyama for the rest of the series, the Timberwolves are in serious trouble.

items.blurb: The 'Most 25-point NBA Playoff games' quiz — How many of the NBA franchise single-season leaders in 25-point playoff games can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: ‘Going to have to really dig in’: Nurse on how Sixers can flip script
items.blurb: ‘Going to have to really dig in’: Nurse on how Sixers can flip script — Philadelphia 76ers head coach Nick Nurse meets with the media after the team’s loss to the New York Knicks that has them down 3-0 in the Eastern Conference Semifinals and talks about what his team can do to climb back into the series.

items.headline: ‘One possession at a time’: Knicks head coach Brown on mentality of group
items.blurb: ‘One possession at a time’: Knicks head coach Brown on mentality of group — New York Knicks head coach Mike Brown meets with the media after the Knicks went up 3-0 on the Philadelphia 76ers in the Eastern Conference Semifinals and talks similarities of teams that he has coached in the past to this Knicks team.

items.headline: Pistons' Tobias Harris remaking his playoff reputation
items.blurb: Pistons' Tobias Harris remaking his playoff reputation — The Detroit Pistons have won five straight playoff games and their veteran forward has been a huge part of it.

items.blurb: Donovan Mitchell, James Harden speak out after Cavaliers' Game 2 loss to Pistons — The Cleveland Cavaliers fell for a second time to the Detroit Pistons in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.headline: Joel Embiid got embarrassed after taunting Mitchell Robinson in Game 3
items.blurb: Joel Embiid got embarrassed after taunting Mitchell Robinson in Game 3 — New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson got the last laugh on Joel Embiid as things got spicy in Game 3 of their Eastern Conference semifinals series.

items.headline: Timberwolves’ Edwards cashes transition triple at first-quarter buzzer
items.blurb: Timberwolves’ Edwards cashes transition triple at first-quarter buzzer — The Minnesota Timberwolves’ Anthony Edwards gets the pass from Rudy Gobert and pulls up in transition and nails the deep three-pointer to beat the buzzer at the end of the first quarter in Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals.

items.headline: Nuggets Will Consider All Options This Offseason Except Trading Nikola Jokic
items.blurb: Nuggets Will Consider All Options This Offseason Except Trading Nikola Jokic — Denver Nuggets president Josh Kroenke declared Friday that "everything is on the table" this offseason following the team's first-round playoff exit, with the clear exception that they will not trade Nikole Jokic.
